caption: Fingerprint
description: The Fingerprint object provides detailed information about a fingerprint, which is a compact representation of data used to identify a longer piece of information, such as a public key, file content, or application implementation. It contains the algorithm or scheme and value of the fingerprint, enabling efficient and reliable identification of the associated data.
name: fingerprint

algorithm.description: The algorithm or scheme used to create the fingerprint, normalized to the caption of algorithm_id. In the case of Other, it is defined by the event source.

algorithm_id.description: The identifier of the normalized algorithm or scheme, which was used to create the fingerprint.

1.caption: MD5
1.description: MD5 message-digest algorithm producing a 128-bit (16-byte) hash value.

2.caption: SHA-1
2.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 1 producing a 160-bit (20-byte) hash value.

3.caption: SHA-256
3.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 2 producing a 256-bit (32-byte) hash value.

4.caption: SHA-512
4.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 2 producing a 512-bit (64-byte) hash value.

5.caption: CTPH
5.description: The ssdeep generated fuzzy checksum. Also known as Context Triggered Piecewise Hash (CTPH).

7.caption: quickXorHash
7.description: Microsoft simple non-cryptographic hash algorithm that works by XORing the bytes in a circular-shifting fashion.

8.caption: SHA-224
8.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 2 producing a 224-bit (28-byte) hash value.

9.caption: SHA-384
9.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 2 producing a 384-bit (48-byte) hash value.

10.caption: SHA-512/224
10.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 2 producing a 512-bit (64-byte) hash value truncated to a 224-bit (28-byte) hash value.

11.caption: SHA-512/256
11.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 2 producing a 512-bit (64-byte) hash value truncated to a 256-bit (32-byte) hash value.

12.caption: SHA3-224
12.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 3 producing a 224-bit (28-byte) hash value.

13.caption: SHA3-256
13.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 3 producing a 256-bit (32-byte) hash value.

14.caption: SHA3-384
14.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 3 producing a 384-bit (48-byte) hash value.

15.caption: SHA3-512
15.description: Secure Hash Algorithm 3 producing a 512-bit (64-byte) hash value.

16.caption: xxHash H3 64-bit
16.description: xxHash H3 producing a 64-bit hash value.

17.caption: xxHash H3 128-bit
17.description: xxHash H3 producing a 128-bit hash value.

18.caption: Imphash
18.description: Import hash (imphash) based on the import table of a Portable Executable (PE) file producing a 128-bit (16-byte) hash value.

19.caption: NPF
19.description: Network Protocol Fingerprint (NPF) used to identify network protocols and applications.

20.caption: HASSH
20.description: HASSH is a network fingerprinting standard which can be used to identify specific SSH client and server implementations.

encoding.description: The encoding of the value attribute, normalized to the caption of encoding_id. In the case of Other, it is defined by the event source.

encoding_id.description: The normalized identifier of the encoding used to represent the fingerprint bytes as the string in value. A verifier must decode value using this encoding to recover the raw hash bytes.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The encoding of the fingerprint value is not known.

1.caption: Hex
1.description: The fingerprint value is encoded as a lowercase or uppercase hexadecimal string.

2.caption: Base64
2.description: The fingerprint value is encoded using standard Base64.

3.caption: Base64URL
3.description: The fingerprint value is encoded using URL- and filename-safe Base64.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The encoding of the fingerprint value is not mapped. See the encoding attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

serialization.description: The canonical serialization scheme used to produce the deterministic byte sequence that was fingerprinted, normalized to the caption of serialization_id. In the case of Other, it is defined by the event source.

serialization_id.description: The identifier of the normalized canonical serialization scheme used to produce the deterministic byte sequence that was fingerprinted. A verifier must apply the same scheme to reproduce the fingerprinted input. Only meaningful where the fingerprinted data is structured, such as an event; fingerprints of opaque data, such as file content, do not populate it. Distinct from algorithm_id, which identifies how the resulting bytes were hashed.

1.caption: JCS
1.description: JSON Canonicalization Scheme - deterministic JSON serialization of the structured data prior to hashing.

2.caption: JWS
2.description: JSON Web Signature - the JWS Signing Input, i.e. BASE64URL(protected header) '.' BASE64URL(payload).

3.caption: COSE
3.description: CBOR Object Signing and Encryption - the CBOR Sig_structure used as the binary input.

4.caption: DSSE
4.description: Dead Simple Signing Envelope - the PAE (Pre-Authentication Encoding) canonical input. Note: DSSE is a signing-envelope scheme, not a hash algorithm; the hash algorithm is carried in algorithm_id.

5.caption: Authenticode
5.description: The Portable Executable (PE) Image Hash procedure that canonicalizes the PE structure into the byte sequence fed to the digest, excluding specified header fields and digesting sections in a defined order.

references.description: Windows Authenticode Portable Executable Signature Format (Microsoft, 2008)

6.caption: Code Signing
6.description: The procedure that describes how hashes of code and other structures form the Code Directory message that is digested.

7.caption: App Package
7.description: The Windows Application Package signing procedure that describes how file hashes are stored in the manifest, and how that manifest is signed.

value.description:
The fingerprint value.

Note: This uses type file_hash_t ("Hash"), which has been generalized for all fingerprints but retains the same name and caption for backwards compatibility.
